D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Spherical Aberration — Marginal vs Paraxial Focus

Spherical surfaces focus marginal (rim) rays closer than paraxial (centre) rays; the longitudinal gap LSA grows as h⁴ — fixed by aspheric or aplanatic lens shapes

For a thin lens with focal length f, marginal focal point shifts by ΔLSA ≈ h²·k(n) where k depends on the lens bending. Stopping down (smaller aperture) reduces SA quadratically. Mirror telescopes use parabolic primaries; camera lenses use aspheric elements (3+ per modern fast prime).

Slide aperture h to see longitudinal SA spread.
